title: Briza rotundata
taxonomy: Briza rotundata is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Briza and the subfamily Pooideae. The species Briza rotundata is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. This species is also known as the 'Round-headed Quaking-grass' due to its unique seed heads that quiver in the breeze. Briza rotundata is often found in well-drained soils and is considered an ornamental plant due to its delicate appearance.
uses: Briza rotundata is also known as the bluebunch sedge, but information regarding its medicinal uses is limited. However, its grass family counterparts have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
